column32: No, I do not have any suggestions.
For the vibration motor method, the feedback was a bit lagging behind; for the pin actuator method, the finger pose detection was a bit jittering that affected the user experience.
Thank you for inviting me to such an interesting demonstration and experiment. I felt that the feedback from the pin actuator was slightly delayed.

- Sometimes the thumb would receive feedback, but the index finger wouldn't receive any feedback. --> Maybe calibrate the vibration motor/pin actuators?
- It might be better to have the user's hand placed a fixed distance from the camera (since the robot gripper depends on the perceived distance between the fingers)
- For this questionnaire, have "1: Strongly Disagree, 5: Strongly Agree"
For the pin actuator, despite it being on the fingertip which makes the vibration more noticeable, the reaction times feels a bit slower.

The camera seemed to have trouble tracking my fingertips with the pin actuators attached. Maybe if the camera retained the finger tracking accuracy when the pin actuator was used, it could have resulted in better sense of contact. I also thought the pin actuator didn't give a strong enough feedback compared to the vibration motors.

Vibration motor feels a bit more reactive in terms of telling us that we are in contact with the object, but pin actuator might be better if the main goal is to hold (to apply pressure) and/or lift the object.

In second part of the task with deformable cube and with no feedback assistant, sensor was sometimes not working properly in reading the data. For example, the cube was pinched until it became quite flat for around 1 second, yet retrieved data only showed that the action was merely 0.2 second.
